(embedded source map)\n```\nThis pattern is common in index files to aggregate modules for easier imports. The embedded source map is also typical in transpiled or bundled code for debugging.\n\n---\n\n### Confirmed Findings:\n- **Functionality:** The code re-exports modules from local files, facilitating modular design.\n- **Source Map:** The embedded source map is standard in development or bundled code, aiding debugging.\n- **Malicious Activity:** No evidence of malicious code, backdoors, or suspicious behavior.\n- **Data Flow & Sinks:** No data processing, user input handling, or external communication.\n- **Obfuscation:** The code is straightforward; no obfuscation detected.\n- **Vulnerabilities:** No vulnerabilities are apparent in this static re-export pattern.\n\n---\n\n### Review of Each Report:\n\n#### Report 1\n- **Assessment:** No issues detected; source map comment is normal.\n- **Scores:** malware=0, securityRisk=0, confidence=1\n- **Verdict:** Correct.
